methods.name: Stage-Progression

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $700M - $6.0B.

private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series C), post-money $700M

data.narrative:
KoBold Metals is a Series C climate and energy company founded by Kurt House, Josh Goldman, and Jeff Jurinak, having raised $1B+ to date. The company's last post-money valuation stands at $3.0B. The peer universe comprises 9 comparable companies—1 public (Linamar, ticker LNZA) and 8 private entities across the climate and energy sector at Series C and adjacent stages, including Electric Hydrogen, Amogy, Redwood Materials, Helion, and QuantumScape.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using a typical Series C-to-next-stage multiplier of 1.7x applied to the $3.0B last post-money, implies a valuation of $5.0B. Peer-median analysis across three same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables—Electric Hydrogen ($1.0B), Amogy ($700M), and Redwood Materials ($6.0B)—yields a median valuation of $1.0B, with a range of $700M to $6.0B.

The triangulated analysis produces a low-to-high range of $850M to $5.8B, with a median of $3.0B. The current $3.0B valuation aligns with the triangulation median but sits above the peer-median method at $1.0B, suggesting valuation depends on stage-progression assumptions and company-specific quality relative to peers. Key uncertainties include the relevance of the Redwood Materials outlier at $6.0B and whether stage-progression multipliers hold across heterogeneous energy subsectors.
